title: Chocolate truffles
subtitle: Dark chocolate ganache truffles rolled in cocoa powder
excerpt: Rich dark chocolate truffles made from a simple ganache, chilled until firm enough to roll, and coated in cocoa powder. The ganache is three ingredients. Makes about twenty-five.
sourceNotes: Chocolate truffles made from a ganache of cream and chocolate are documented in French confectionery from the early twentieth century, named for their resemblance to the truffle fungus when rolled in cocoa powder. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg: a chocolate cream made from melted chocolate, cream, and butter is documented in the sweetmeat chapter. Interwar French domestic confectionery guides (pre-1928, public domain) document the ganache-based truffle in its modern form, including the tempering note on dipping.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze coated truffles in a single layer, then bag once solid, for up to 1 month.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keep refrigerated for up to 2 weeks. Remove 20 minutes before serving for the ganache to soften.

glossaryTerms.definition: An emulsion of cream and chocolate, made by pouring hot cream over finely chopped chocolate and stirring from the centre outward until smooth. The ratio of cream to chocolate determines the texture: a 1:1 ratio produces a pourable ganache for glazing; a 1:2 ratio (one part cream to two parts chocolate) produces a firm ganache that sets solid when chilled and can be rolled into truffles. Butter is often added for gloss and softness. The emulsion can split if the temperature difference between the cream and chocolate is too large; warming the chocolate slightly before adding the cream prevents this.

content.text: rolled into a ball and coated. The quality depends entirely on the chocolate; use one you would eat. The rolling can be done quickly and messily — the cocoa coating covers the imperfections.

Place the {{dark-chocolate}} in a heatproof bowl. Bring the {{double-cream}} to a simmer in a small saucepan, then pour over the chocolate. Leave for 1 minute. Stir from the centre outward in slow, tight circles until the ganache is completely smooth. Stir in the {{unsalted-butter}}, {{vanilla-extract}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Pour into a shallow dish and refrigerate for at least 2 hours until firm enough to scoop.

Spread the {{cocoa-powder}} on a plate. Use a small ice-cream scoop or two teaspoons to portion the ganache into 12–15 g pieces. Roll briefly between your palms into a rough ball; the warmth of your hands will melt the surface slightly, which helps the cocoa adhere. Roll immediately in the cocoa powder to coat all sides. Set on a tray lined with baking paper. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
